scope: Adversarial probes of TLS boundary map for owner-outcome leakage, runtime mutation drift, and authority-shaped naming.
method: Static review of the boundary map's forbidden patterns, safe vocabulary, and owner-handoff rules against eight abuse cases that try to push TLS over the boundary.

abuse_cases.title: TLS artifact named like a CDE outcome
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Emit a TLS artifact named 'tls_closure_decision_outcome.json' that mimics a CDE closure outcome.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map rejects the artifact name via forbidden_artifact_name_patterns and routes the request to CDE.
abuse_cases.observed_result: Pattern '*control_decision*' is listed but generic '*closure_decision*' is not. The wildcard '*decision*' would catch it but is not in the list. Marginal coverage.
abuse_cases.finding: Forbidden artifact-name pattern list is too narrow. Closure, judgment, readiness, and final-decision artifact names can slip through.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Add forbidden patterns: '*closure_decision*', '*readiness_decision*', '*final_decision*', '*judgment*', '*outcome*', and broaden authority-shape coverage.

abuse_cases.title: TLS artifact named like SEL enforcement
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Emit a TLS artifact named 'tls_enforcement_record.json' or 'tls_enforced_action.json' that mimics SEL enforcement.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map rejects the name via '*enforcement*' pattern and routes the responsibility to SEL.
abuse_cases.observed_result: '*enforcement*' is listed and matches. The verb form 'enforce' / 'enforced' is partially covered by the wildcard but not separately enumerated.
abuse_cases.finding: Wildcard catches the obvious case but explicit verb-form patterns improve clarity.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Add '*enforce*' (verb-stem) explicitly to forbidden patterns; add SEL canonical-owner reference in the rejection message guidance.

abuse_cases.title: TLS artifact containing approval language
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Embed approval-shaped fields ('approver', 'approved_at', 'approval_status') inside a TLS owner-input packet.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map flags approval-shaped fields and replaces with safe vocabulary (review_request, advisory_result).
abuse_cases.observed_result: Forbidden artifact-name pattern '*approval*' exists, but the boundary map does not enumerate forbidden FIELD names. Field-level leakage is uncovered.
abuse_cases.finding: Boundary map lacks a forbidden_field_patterns section. Approval-shaped, certification-shaped, promotion-shaped, and decision-shaped fields can leak inside artifacts whose names are safe.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Add 'forbidden_field_patterns' that mirror the artifact-name patterns; add safe replacements (review_request, advisory_result, review_observation) per cluster.

abuse_cases.title: TLS artifact containing certification language
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Set a TLS field 'certification_status' = 'certified' inside an evidence attachment.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map blocks the field and replaces with 'readiness_evidence' or 'certification_input' under the certification cluster.
abuse_cases.observed_result: '*certification*' covers names but not field values. The field-level absence (see RT-BND-03) is the same root.
abuse_cases.finding: No field-level guard. Certification-shaped values pass.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Add 'forbidden_field_patterns' and 'forbidden_value_patterns' (certified, promoted, approved, blocked) with safe replacements (readiness_evidence, evidence_observation).

abuse_cases.title: TLS artifact containing promotion language
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Mark a TLS observation as 'promotion_ready' = true.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map blocks 'promotion_ready' and replaces with 'readiness_signal'.
abuse_cases.observed_result: Pattern '*promotion*' exists; '*promoted*' exists. 'promotion_ready' as a name pattern is captured. As a field value, see RT-BND-04 root cause.
abuse_cases.finding: Name pattern works; field/value pattern does not.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Same as RT-BND-04 (forbidden_field_patterns and forbidden_value_patterns). Add 'readiness_signal' as the explicit safe replacement.

abuse_cases.title: TLS dashboard text implying TLS decides
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Insert a dashboard string 'TLS has decided to fix X first' into a TLS-emitted summary.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map blocks decide/decision verbiage in TLS-owned text and substitutes 'TLS recommends X first'.
abuse_cases.observed_result: Boundary map does not list a dashboard-text vocabulary rule; the 'safe_vocabulary' list is general but is not bound to text-rendering surfaces.
abuse_cases.finding: Dashboard rendering text is uncovered. TLS-owned text could imply TLS decides.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Add 'forbidden_text_phrases' for TLS-owned text: 'TLS decides', 'TLS approves', 'TLS enforces', 'TLS promotes', 'TLS certifies'. Add safe replacements: 'TLS recommends', 'TLS observes', 'TLS signals', 'TLS prioritizes'.

abuse_cases.title: TLS action plan implying runtime mutation
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Emit a TLS action plan that says 'will execute migration X' or 'will mutate runtime state Y'.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map blocks runtime-mutation verbs in TLS action plans; only recommendation phrasing is allowed.
abuse_cases.observed_result: Boundary map declares 'runtime_execution_mutation' as forbidden responsibility but does not define forbidden action-verb phrases (execute, mutate, deploy, rollback, freeze, block).
abuse_cases.finding: Action-plan verbs are not constrained; 'recommendation packet' could carry runtime-mutation verbs and look legitimate.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Add 'forbidden_action_verbs' for TLS-emitted action-plan content: 'execute', 'mutate', 'deploy', 'promote', 'rollback', 'freeze', 'block', 'enforce', 'approve', 'certify'. Require imperative TLS verbs to be 'recommend', 'observe', 'rank', 'classify', 'flag', 'attach_evidence'.

abuse_cases.title: TLS owner-input packet using owner-outcome fields
abuse_cases.attempted_violation: Place owner-outcome fields ('control_decision', 'enforcement_action', 'certification_outcome', 'promotion_outcome', 'approval_outcome') inside a TLS owner-input packet body.
abuse_cases.expected_result: Boundary map distinguishes owner_input vs owner_outcome at the field level and rejects owner-outcome fields inside TLS owner-input packets.
abuse_cases.observed_result: Boundary map's owner-handoff rules state owner-input vs owner-outcome at the role level but do not enumerate owner-input vs owner-outcome FIELD sets.
abuse_cases.finding: Owner-input vs owner-outcome distinction is asserted at the rule level but not at the field level. A TLS packet can claim recommendation_only=true while embedding owner-outcome fields.
abuse_cases.recommended_fix: Add an 'owner_input_packet_field_policy' section: an allowed_owner_input_fields list (observation, signal, recommendation, ranking, classification, evidence_ref, replay_ref, owner_input_observation, recommendation_only, owner_outcome_present, schema_version), and a forbidden_owner_outcome_fields list (control_decision, enforcement_action, certification_outcome, promotion_outcome, approval_outcome, authority_decision). Require packets to assert owner_outcome_present=false.
